saudi_arabia_crude: Four major offshore fields remain shut since Mar 9, but Yanbu loadings have surged 220% to 2.2 mb/d as Saudi Arabia reroutes crude via the East-West Pipeline to bypass Hormuz; overall exports rose to ~7.37 mb/d, the highest since April 2023 — Bloomberg citing Kpler.
saudi_arabia_lng: Saudi Arabia has no meaningful LNG export capacity; the Jafurah unconventional gas development is in early construction and the conflict has underscored the kingdom's strategic urgency to diversify beyond crude oil — Rystad Energy.
saudi_arabia_lpg: Saudi LPG exports have been curtailed by reduced NGL throughput from offshore field closures and the Mar 2-13 Ras Tanura shutdown; volumes partially rerouted via Yanbu are not fully offsetting the ~20-25% crude production cut — Kpler.
uae_crude: UAE crude output has fallen by more than half as the Hormuz closure forces massive shut-ins; ADNOC is routing residual exports via the 1 mb/d ADCOP pipeline to Fujairah, but drone attacks on Fujairah port have disrupted loading — US News citing ADNOC.
uae_lng: Das Island LNG is operating at minimal capacity due to the Hormuz blockage; Habshan gas complex (60% of UAE domestic gas) resumed Mar 23 after a 4-day emergency shutdown, restoring feed gas to remaining operations — S&P Global Platts.
uae_lpg: UAE LPG exports from Ruwais and Habshan are severely disrupted: Habshan resumed Mar 23 but Shah Gas Field remains shut and Ruwais Refinery-2 (a major NGL source) is offline after drone strikes — Kpler vessel tracking.
iraq_crude: Iraq declared FM on all foreign-operated oilfields Mar 20; Basra output has collapsed to ~800 kb/d from 3.3 mb/d as storage fills and southern terminals are halted — BP ordered to cut North Rumaila to 350 kb/d — Reuters.
iraq_lng: Iraq has no LNG export infrastructure; the Basra Gas Company's flaring-to-capture projects are stalled as associated gas production has collapsed alongside the 76% crude output reduction — Rystad Energy.
iraq_lpg: Iraq's LPG exports, linked to Basra Gas Company processing, are effectively halted following the FM declaration and shutdown of Basra export terminals — IEA Oil Market Report.
kuwait_crude: KPC declared FM on crude exports; the Al-Ahmadi refinery (730 kb/d) was struck by Iranian drones on Mar 19-20 with fires across multiple units, and Kafco fuel storage was destroyed — Kuwait News Agency.
kuwait_lng: Kuwait's Al-Zour LNG import FSRU, critical for domestic power generation, faces acute supply uncertainty as Qatar's LNG is entirely offline and Asian spot LNG prices have surged ~39% — Argus Media.
kuwait_lpg: Kuwait's LPG exports, linked to NGL extraction at the Al-Ahmadi refinery complex, are disrupted after the refinery was struck by drones on Mar 19-20 and multiple processing units were shut — Kpler.
iran_crude: USAF struck Kharg Island military targets on Mar 13, sparing oil infrastructure but warning crude facilities could be hit next; Kharg handles 94% of Iran's ~1.68 mb/d crude exports, primarily to China — TIME citing Kpler.
iran_lng: Iran has no LNG export capacity despite the world's second-largest gas reserves; Israeli strikes on South Pars Phases 3-6, 14, 17-18 on Mar 18 took ~12% of Iran's gas output offline — S&P Global Platts.
iran_lpg: Iran's LPG exports, already sanctions-constrained and routed primarily to China via ship-to-ship transfers, face further disruption from the Hormuz closure and ~30% shut-in of southern NGL-linked oil fields — Vortexa.
qatar_crude: All Qatar offshore oil fields (~440 kb/d including Al Shaheen) have been shut since early March along with Laffan Refineries 1 and 2; QatarEnergy's FM encompasses all hydrocarbon exports — Bloomberg.
qatar_lng: All 77 Mtpa of Qatar's LNG production halted since Mar 2, removing ~20% of global LNG supply; Asian spot prices surged past $25/MMBtu (+39%) and European TTF spiked 52% within 24 hours; FM extended to Italy, Belgium, South Korea, and China on Mar 24 — Reuters citing Kpler.
qatar_lpg: Qatar's LPG exports, entirely a byproduct of LNG production at Ras Laffan, have been halted since Mar 2 along with all downstream operations — restart is impossible until the conflict ends — QatarEnergy statement.
oman_crude: Oman's inland oil production is largely intact despite Salalah port oil tanks being struck by Iranian drones on Mar 11; Mina Al-Fahal terminal remains operational, and Oman's non-Hormuz position gives it a strategic export advantage — Argus Media.
oman_lng: Oman LNG's 10.4 Mtpa facility, located outside the Hormuz chokepoint, is one of the few Middle Eastern LNG operations still running; with Qatar's 77 Mtpa offline, Oman's cargoes are commanding significant Asian spot premiums — Platts LNG Daily.
oman_lpg: Oman's LPG exports from Fahud and Saih Rawl gas processing are largely unaffected by the Gulf conflict; inland production and the non-Hormuz export position provide relative insulation from regional disruption — Kpler.
bahrain_crude: BAPCO Sitra refinery (405 kb/d, just completed a $7.3B upgrade) was struck by Iranian drones on Mar 4 and Mar 9; Bapco Energies declared FM on all group operations — the facility is offline indefinitely pending structural assessment — Argus Media.
bahrain_lng: Bahrain, an LNG importer dependent on Saudi pipeline gas, faces acute energy security risk as the conflict disrupts regional gas supply chains and the Sitra refinery destruction has eliminated domestic refining capacity — Gulf News.
bahrain_lpg: Bahrain's small LPG exports, linked to Bapco refinery byproduct streams, have ceased entirely following the destruction of Sitra refinery and the FM declaration on all Bapco Energies operations — Argus Media.
russia_crude: China now absorbs 52% of Russia's fossil fuel export revenue (Feb 2026); ESPO crude trades above the G7 price cap with 68% of Russian crude moving on shadow-fleet tankers, making Russia a key beneficiary of Gulf supply disruption — CREA citing Kpler.
russia_lng: Russia's Yamal and Arctic LNG-2 projects are positioned to capture marginal demand as Qatar's 77 Mtpa shutdown has created the largest LNG supply deficit in history; Asian spot demand for Russian cargoes is surging — ICIS LNG Edge.
russia_lpg: Russia's LPG exports remain a small share of global trade; the Gulf conflict has marginally improved Russian NGL competitiveness in Asian markets as Middle Eastern supply from Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Qatar has been curtailed — Argus Media.
us_crude: The US is contributing 172 mb (43% of the IEA's historic 400 mb coordinated release), with first deliveries from Mar 20 over 120 days; Brent surged to $126/bbl before easing to ~$102 with an $18/bbl geopolitical risk premium embedded — Bloomberg citing Goldman Sachs.
us_lng: US LNG exports, already the world's largest, are the primary beneficiary of Qatar's total shutdown — the ~20% global LNG supply gap has driven Asian spot prices up ~39% and European gas up ~50%, pulling maximum utilisation from Sabine Pass, Freeport, and Plaquemines — Platts.
us_lpg: US Gulf Coast LPG exports from Enterprise and Targa terminals are benefiting from sharply higher Asian demand as Saudi, UAE, and Qatari NGL-linked supply has been curtailed; the LPG-naphtha spread favours increased US exports — Argus LPG World.
china_crude: China ramped crude imports 16% in Jan-Feb 2026 to ~12.47 mb/d, stockpiling ahead of the Hormuz crisis; pipeline diversification (Russia, Kazakhstan) covers only 12-15% of total imports, leaving 40-50% of seaborne flows Hormuz-dependent — Bloomberg citing Columbia CGEP.
china_lng: QatarEnergy extended FM to Chinese LNG contracts on Mar 24; Wanhua Chemical has declared FM on Middle East supplies, and spot LNG procurement costs have surged ~39% — pipeline gas via Power of Siberia provides partial insulation — Reuters.
china_lpg: China, the world's largest LPG importer, faces tightening supply as Middle Eastern NGL-linked LPG from Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Qatar is offline; PDH plant operators are scrambling for US and Australian cargoes at sharply higher prices — ICIS.
india_crude: India is aggressively shifting crude sourcing to Russian barrels; MRPL declared FM on gasoline exports after cutting 500 kb/d of refinery runs due to Gulf supply disruption, and surging Brent is compressing refinery margins — Platts.
india_lng: Qatar supplied 41.4% of India's LNG imports (11.2 MT/yr from Ras Laffan); Petronet LNG declared FM after the halt and Gujarat Gas imposed 50% supply cuts to industrial customers — India is actively negotiating alternative US and Australian cargoes — Business Standard.
india_lpg: India's LPG imports, essential for the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala cooking fuel subsidy programme, face supply risk as Middle Eastern cargoes are disrupted; the government may need to source replacement volumes from the US Gulf Coast at significantly higher prices — Economic Times.
japan_crude: Japan released 80 mb from strategic reserves (54 mb crude + 26 mb products) as part of the IEA's record release; Middle East crude dependence stands at 93.5%, making Japan the most exposed major importer — Japan Times citing METI.
japan_lng: Japan faces acute LNG supply risk from Qatar's total shutdown and is relying on increased US spot cargoes and Australian contract volumes; nuclear restart progress provides some demand-side relief but winter exposure remains a concern — Japan Times citing METI.
japan_lpg: Japan's LPG imports for petrochemical and residential heating face tightened supply and surging prices as Middle Eastern LPG from Saudi Arabia and Qatar is offline; diversification to US and Australian cargoes is underway at significant cost premium — Argus Media.
south_korea_crude: South Korea's large refining complex (SK, S-Oil, GS Caltex, Hyundai) is exposed to Middle East supply disruption; the SPR release of 22.5 mb (5 mb released by Mar 25) is bridging the gap; YNCC has declared FM on petrochemical products — Yonhap.
south_korea_lng: South Korea sees no LNG shortages despite the Qatar FM, supported by diversified long-term contracts (80% of 46.7 MT imported in 2025 under term deals); KOGAS signed a new 10-year deal with Trafigura for 3.3 mtpa of mostly US LNG — S&P Global.
south_korea_lpg: South Korea's LPG imports, feeding naphtha-alternative petrochemical crackers, are disrupted by the Middle Eastern supply shutdown; the LPG-naphtha price spread has shifted as both feedstocks face Gulf-linked supply constraints — ICIS.
thailand_crude: Thailand's diversified crude sourcing provides relative insulation from the Gulf crisis, though rising Brent prices are increasing import costs and compressing domestic refinery margins — Bangkok Post citing EPPO.
thailand_lng: Thailand's growing LNG import dependency, driven by declining domestic gas from the Gulf of Thailand and Myanmar pipeline, is tested by the conflict as spot LNG prices have surged well beyond pre-crisis levels — S&P Global.
thailand_lpg: Thailand's small LPG imports for petrochemical feedstock and cooking fuel face higher prices as global supply tightens, though relatively low Middle East LPG dependence limits direct exposure compared to larger Asian importers — Argus Media.
vietnam_crude: Kuwait supplies ~80% of Vietnam's crude imports (primarily for Nghi Son Refinery), creating acute Hormuz exposure; Vietnam has cut its MFN oil import tariff to 0% in response to the energy crisis — VietnamNet citing MOIT.
vietnam_lng: Vietnam received its first 2026 LNG cargo of 63,000 tons at Thi Vai terminal in late February; PV GAS confirms supply through April but plans for a second FSRU at Vung Tau (+14 million Sm3/day) face headwinds from surging spot prices — Vietnam.vn citing PV GAS.
vietnam_lpg: Vietnam's LPG imports for cooking fuel and petrochemical markets face higher procurement costs as Middle Eastern supply disruption tightens the regional balance, though small volumes limit exposure relative to major Asian importers — ICIS.
